title: Notching and tagging piglets
subtitle: Identification requirements for pigs kept under UK pig identification regulations.
excerpt: UK pig keepers must identify all pigs before movement off the holding. Ear notching gives permanent litter and individual identification; tags carrying the CPH number satisfy movement document requirements. This covers the legal requirements, the notching procedure, and tag application.
sourceNotes: Animal and Plant Health Agency (APHA), pig identification rules for England (2021 update). DEFRA Pig Identification Regulations. National Pig Association, piglet identification guidance.

content.text: UK pig keepers are legally required to identify all pigs before movement from the holding of birth.
done at one to three days of age provides permanent identification. Tags carrying the
are required for movement documents. Check the current APHA regulations for England, Wales, or Scotland as rules differ slightly between nations.

Notch at one to three days of age. Have a clean, dry work surface and disinfect the notcher between piglets with iodine solution. Restrain the piglet firmly, hold the ear flat, and apply the V-notch cleanly in one firm squeeze. Partial notches that tear rather than cut are difficult to read later; use sharp pliers and apply full pressure in one motion. The notching code varies by herd record system: standard UK systems use different positions of the right and left ear for litter and individual number. Record the code used in the herd records.

Metal slap marks or plastic ear tags can be applied at any age before first movement. Apply tags to the middle third of the ear between the major ear veins. Tags applied in the lower third of the ear are prone to tearing out. Confirm the tag's CPH number is legible before the pig leaves the holding.

attrs.body: You must be registered with APHA as a pig keeper before keeping pigs; a CPH number and a herd number are both required. The herd number appears on movement documents and ear tags. Registration is free via APHA.
attrs.title: Register before keeping pigs
